title: Aframax rates could face further weakening in Q2 2024
section_path: Aframax rates could face further weakening in Q2 2024
section_path_text: Aframax rates could face further weakening in Q2 2024
summary:
Main
Seasonal factors in the Atlantic Basin and increasing sanctioning activity could play a vital role in shaping the trajectory of freight rates in Q2 2024
By Ioannis Papadimitriou
Crude tanker rates have remained quite healthy for owners in the last couple of years, especially post the Russian invasion and the changes to trade flow and fleet behaviour patterns that followed suit. However, seasonal and regulatory downside risks could be on the cards in the months to follow for Aframax tankers with rates currently hovering below 2023 averages, according to Vortexa Tanker Basket composed from Baltic Exchanges freight assessments. Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-04-11 101408.png | assets/2024-04-11_aframax-rates-could-face-further-weakening-in-q2-2024_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_d941aa3bd47a.png
The bedrock of Aframax employment has mainly been the transatlantic US Gulf-to-Europe crude trade.
